items.title: Europe’s defense boom faces a new test: Can it actually deliver weapons?
items.description: Europe’s defense boom faces a test as investors weigh whether budgets and backlogs can become weapons, factories and returns.

items.title: Xi touts China Communist Party's global influence in speech marking 105th anniversary
items.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping spoke Wednesday to commemorate the 105th anniversary of the ruling Communist Party of China.

items.title: LNG market disruption may continue for months as a top producer withholds some Italian shipments
items.description: QatarEnergy extended its force majeure, withholding four additional LNG cargoes until September, as damage from the war prolongs supply constraints.

items.title: North Sea oil and renewables: The UK’s next PM faces a defining energy policy decision
items.description: Andy Burnham could face an early test over North Sea oil drilling as energy shocks revive the UK energy security debate.

items.title: KKR to control South Korea's $1.3 billion renewables platform with SK as AI power demand rises
items.description: The announcement came after South Korea announced on Monday three massive investment projects, spanning semiconductors, physical AI and AI data centers.

items.title: Japan spent $74 billion propping up the yen. Investors say the real battle is with the Fed
items.description: Yen intervention alone is unlikely to reverse losses while wide U.S.-Japan rate differentials keep supporting the dollar, said analysts.

items.title: Employers who laid off workers citing AI are already starting to regret it
items.description: Companies are realizing artificial intelligence can't do everything after all, prompting them to rehire employees to grow their businesses

items.title: Anthropic says Trump admin has lifted export controls on Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5
items.description: The U.S. Department of Commerce lifted the export controls on Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models

items.title: Iran says it is selling oil at 20% premium as end of U.S. blockade sees 40 million barrels exported
items.description: The ceasefire prompted a surge in crude shipments through the Strait of Hormuz, where traffic had largely ground to a halt during the conflict.

items.title: CNBC Daily Open: AI demand fuels investors' portfolios while oil posts biggest monthly decline
items.description: The tech space continues to be fueled by rising artificial intelligence demand, with investors piling their portfolios in the technology, while Amazon Web Services dabbles with Forward Deployed Engine

items.title: Why Asia still struggles to create its own mega-IPOs like SpaceX
items.description: Asia has no shortage of entrepreneurs, engineers or giant domestic markets. Yet when it comes to producing the kind of blockbuster listings seen in the U.S., the region continues to lag.

items.title: After a soft Nike quarter, we have a big decision to make on the stock
items.description: The results are unlikely to improve Nike's standing in the portfolio

items.title: China-linked actors target more than technology as AI competition with U.S. intensifies
items.description: Cyberattacks from China-based entities are on the rise and not just targeting specific tech, analysts warn, as the U.S. AI race intensifies.

items.title: Trump's annual financial disclosure shows more than $580M in crypto-related income
items.description: Trump's 927-page financial disclosure report for 2025 totals more than 900 pages detailing holdings in cryptocurrency and stocks.

items.title: Trump says Council of Economic Advisers head Pierre Yared is leaving
items.description: The Council of Economic Advisers has provided the president with information and advice on both domestic and international economic policy since 1946.

items.title: Record chip rally adds $2 trillion in combined value to Micron, Intel and AMD in second quarter
items.description: Wall Street poured into chipmakers not named Nvidia in the second quarter, as the artificial intelligence boom expanded to include more suppliers.

items.title: Social Security gender gap means women receive about $4,800 less in annual benefits. What to know before claiming
items.description: Women tend to earn less and spend more time outside of the workforce, which may reduce their Social Security benefits. Here's what to consider before claiming.

items.title: Jim Cramer says the AI trade has shifted — and these stocks are leading now
items.description: CNBC's Jim Cramer said Wall Street is rewarding the companies supplying the artificial intelligence boom rather than the technology giants funding it.

items.title: Nike results top estimates even as China sales drop 12%; retailer expects $986 million tariff refund
items.description: Nike was expected to report another quarter of declining sales as the sneaker giant struggles to regain strength under its turnaround strategy.

items.title: Michael Burry says he's shorting Caterpillar for the first time after it nearly doubled in the AI-driven rally of 2026
items.description: "Caterpillar jumped out at me," Burry said. "I have never shorted Caterpillar. It has always done great for me on the long side in the past."
